statement.markdown:
Given an array of integers `nums`, remove all occurrences of the maximum element to make the array as small as possible.

Your task is to write a function that returns the minimum possible size of the array.
statement.inputFormat: Input consists of an array of space-separated integers.
statement.notes: You can remove as many elements as you like as long as it's the maximum element.

examples.explanation: We can remove all occurrences of 5 and 9

hints.text: Start from the end of the array

editorial.title: Minimize the Number of Operations
editorial.intuition: We want to remove all occurrences of the maximum element, so start from the end and keep removing it.
editorial.bruteForce: Count the occurrences of each element and remove all occurrences of the maximum one.
editorial.optimalSolution: Start from the end of the array and keep removing the maximum element until it's not found.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: This approach ensures we remove all occurrences of the maximum element, which is the optimal solution.
editorial.dryRun: Start from the end of the array, e.g., if the input is 9 1 2 2 3 1 4 1 1 5, start with 9 and remove it, then move back to 5 and remove it.
commonMistakes: Not starting from the end of the array

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int minOperations(vector<int>& nums); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int minOperations(int[] nums); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def minOperations(self, nums: list[int]) -> int: pass

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int minOperations(vector<int>& nums) { for (int i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++) { if (nums[i] == max_element(nums.begin(), nums.end()) - nums.begin()) nums.erase(nums.begin() + i); } return nums.size(); } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int minOperations(int[] nums) { int max = Integer.MIN_VALUE; for (int i = 0; i < nums.length; i++) max = Math.max(max, nums[i]); for (int i = nums.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) if (nums[i] == max) nums = remove(nums, i); return nums.length; } private static int[] remove(int[] nums, int i) { int[] result = new int[nums.length - 1]; System.arraycopy(nums, 0, result, 0, i); System.arraycopy(nums, i + 1, result, i, nums.length - i - 1); return result; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def minOperations(self, nums: list[int]) -> int: max_val = max(nums) for i in range(len(nums) - 1, -1, -1): if nums[i] == max_val: nums = nums[:i] + nums[i + 1:] return len(nums)
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function minOperations(nums) { var max = Math.max(...nums); for (var i = nums.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) { if (nums[i] == max) nums = nums.slice(0, i).concat(nums.slice(i + 1)); } return nums.length; }

solutions.python: class Solution: def minOperations(self, nums: list[int]) -> int: max_val = nums[0] for i in range(1, len(nums)): max_val = max(max_val, nums[i]) result = 0 for num in nums: if num == max_val: result += 1 return len(nums) - result
solutions.cpp: class Solution { public: int minOperations(vector<int>& nums) { int max_val = nums[0]; int result = 0; for (int i = 1; i < nums.size(); i++) { max_val = max(max_val, nums[i]); if (nums[i] == max_val) result++; } return nums.size() - result; } };
